english: to disperse, ramble, stray C. (= *yan čá-če* (yan cha che) W.); to spring or leap off Cs.; to be scattered Sch.

Glr. a large tributary of the Yangtsekyang coming from the north, in western China, east of the town of Bathang; nevertheless Tibetan historians, from a partiality to old legends, describe it as flowing near the mountain of Yarlhasampo. V. Köpp. II, 50.

a snowy mountain, between Lhasa and the frontier of Bhotan, near which according to tradition the first king of Tibet, rnya-k'ri-btsán-po (rnya khri btsan po), Nyat'i tsáṅpo (nya t'i tsang po), coming from India, first entered the country.

branch, bough, frq., yál-gai tsúl-du (yal ga'i tshul du) ramified S.g., yál-ga-čan (yal ga can) branchy, full of boughs; yal-p'ráṅ (yal phrang) Cs., yál-ga p'reu (yal ga phre'u) Sch. small branch, twig; yal-dáb (yal 'dab) a branch full of leaves Cs.

to dwindle, fail; disappear, vanish, drod yal (drod yal) animal heat (in a living being) diminishes, (an inanimate object) cools down, grows cold; ṅad yal (ngad yal) it evaporates Lt.; of beer: to get stale, dead (W.: *yal čá-če* (yal cha che)); *(s)kug(s) gyál-kan-lu yal ča dug* (skugs gyal mkhan lu yal cha dug) W. the stake is lost in going to the winner; oja yál-ba bžin-du ('ja' yal ba bzhin du) Glr. like the vanishing of the rainbow; yal-šúl (yal shul) Wdn. in a fruit the remnants of the withered blossom; to be obliged to yield, to be dislodged Glr. fol. 25, but perh. the signification: to disappear is also here admissible; lus daṅ srog yal (lus dang srog yal) Dzl. body and soul are trifled away, are lost; *go-yál* (go yal) (v. sgó-po (sgo po)) one who has lost himself by gambling and has thus become the slave of another; yál-bar dór-ba (yal bar dor ba), obór-ba ('bor ba), 1. Sch. to annihilate, annul. 2. Cs. to despise, yžan (gzhan) other people. Cf. yól-ba (yol ba).
Cs. 100 000 octillions, yal-yál čén-po (yal yal chen po) a nonillion; yet cf. dkrigs-pa (dkrigs pa).

from ya (ya), 1. from above, obáb-pa ('bab pa) to come down from above Cs.; above, yás-kyi (yas kyi) the one above, the upper one Do.; yás-nas (yas nas) from above C., yas mas (yas mas), a. from above and from below Cs. b. upward and downward Cs.; yas-byón (yas byon) coming from above Mil. — 2. off, away, yas ytóṅ-ba (yas gtong ba), p'aṅ-yás ytóṅ-ba (phang yas gtong ba), ytor-yas byéd-pa (gtor yas byed pa) Glr. and elsewh., to throw away. — 3. in comp. without, mt'a-yás (mtha' yas) without an end, endless, frq.; bgraṅ-yás (bgrang yas) numberless Gram.

1. num. fig.: 54. — 2. in some combinations inst. of yid (yid), so yi yčód-pa (yi gcod pa) yi(d) čád-pa (yid chad pa) 1. to forget, e.g. a benefactor Glr. 2. more frq. to give up. to despair Dzl.; despondency, despair Mil.; yi-p'rí-ba (yi phri ba) a disliking, hatred Cs.; yi(d)-múg-pa (yid mug pa), yi-múg-par gyúr-ba (yi mug par gyur ba) to despair, frq.; yi-ráṅ-ba (yi rang ba) to be glad, to rejoice, v. ráṅ-ba (rang ba); yi-ysád-pa (yi ysad pa) Cs. = yi-múg-pa (yi mug pa).

appetite, yí-ga ogag (yi ga 'gag), ldog (ldog) the appetite is lost, aversion, disgust is felt, yí-ga sdaṅ (yi ga sdang) id. Sch.; očus ('chus) id. Med.; yi-gar oóṅ (yi gar 'ong) it is grateful to the taste, it tastes well Med.

in comp. yig (yig), 1. letter, yi-ge dbú-čan (yi ge dbu can) (W. *róm-yig* (rom yig)*) the Tibetan printed letters, dbu-méd (dbu med) (W. *t'a-yig* (tha yig)*) current handwriting, of which there are again different kinds: dpé-yig (dpe yig) the more distinct and careful, used in copying books, okyúg-yig ('khyug yig) the cursory and often rather illegible writing in letters, and obam-yig ('bam yig), the very large and regular style invented for the use of elementary writing-schools (v. specimens of all of them in the lithogr. supplement to Cs.'s grammar). -- yi-ge-drúg-pa (yi ge drug pa) the six-syllable (prayer), the Ommanipadmehum Glr. and elsewh.; yi-ge-bdún-pa (yi ge bdun pa) and brgyá-pa (brgya pa) Mil.? yi-ge bsláb-pa (yi ge bslab pa) to learn reading and writing, yig-rtsís (yig rtsis) reading, writing, and cyphering; ká-yig (ka yig) the letter k. — 2. anything that is written, note, card, bill, document; inscription, title (more accurately ká-yig (kha yig)), esp. letter, epistle; yí-ge bžág-pa (yi ge bzhag pa) a deposited document, bond C.; dge-sdíg-gi yí-ge (dge sdig gi yi ge) register of virtues and iniquities; yí-gei lán (yi ge'i lan) a written answer Glr.; yí-gei šubs (yi ge'i shubs) a. envelope, b. lettercase, pocket-book; yi-ge obrī-ba (yi ge 'bri ba) to write a letter, spriṅ-ba (spring ba) W. *kál-če* (kal che)* to send off, fob-
